extraction.article_title: Critical Marimo pre-auth RCE flaw now under active exploitation
attack_chain: Attacker connects to unauthenticated WebSocket terminal endpoint
Attacker gains interactive shell access as the Marimo process user
Attacker executes commands to enumerate host and exfiltrate sensitive data
proposed_phase_updates: Add detection for unauthenticated WebSocket endpoints that expose shell or terminal functionality.
Add checks for /terminal/ws and similar endpoints returning interactive prompt behavior without session authentication.
Prioritize public-facing notebook and data-app platforms that expose developer tooling or terminal bridges.

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: A public WebSocket endpoint exposes an interactive terminal session without enforcing authentication or authorization. If the server upgrades the connection and binds it to a shell, an attacker can interact with the host as the application user.
proposed_kb_patterns.payload:
GET /terminal/ws HTTP/1.1
Host: target.example
Upgrade: websocket
Connection: Upgrade
Sec-WebSocket-Key: dGhlIHNhbXBsZSBub25jZQ==
Sec-WebSocket-Version: 13
Origin: https://target.example
detect: terminal prompt or shell-like banner after upgrade
connection remains open and accepts interactive input

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: Once an unauthenticated terminal session is established, the attacker can execute arbitrary OS commands through the interactive shell tied to the application process.
detect: host fingerprint or system information returned
